raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The artifact makes an explicit contribution claim (chemistry-agnostic, model-free SoH estimation); reconstructing and stress-testing that thesis is a natural part of any 'what do you think' response to a research excerpt.","R1.2":"Several architectural decisions are asserted as causally linked to outcomes (attention layer → chemistry adaptivity; IC-peak tokens → physically meaningful features); their internal logical coherence is a central evaluation target.","R1.3":"Specific quantitative performance claims are made (RMSE 1.8% vs 3.4%/2.9%/2.5%) without accompanying confidence intervals, dataset sizes, or ablation references; aligning those numbers to what the text actually supports is load-bearing under a general review.","R1.4":"The novelty framing ('unlike conventional approaches', 'chemistry-agnostic', 'model-free') is core to the paper's positioning; assessing the delta vs the three cited baselines and any omitted contemporaries is a natural read of 'what do you think' applied to a research claim."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Are you primarily asking whether the technical claims and performance numbers are internally sound and well-supported (soundness / evidence-claim alignment), or whether this work is sufficiently novel and differentiated relative to the cited baselines and the broader literature (novelty / positioning)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The artifact makes an explicit contribution claim (chemistry-agnostic, model-free SoH estimation); reconstructing and stress-testing that thesis is a natural part of any 'what do you think' response to a research excerpt.
rationale.R1.2: Several architectural decisions are asserted as causally linked to outcomes (attention layer → chemistry adaptivity; IC-peak tokens → physically meaningful features); their internal logical coherence is a central evaluation target.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative performance claims are made (RMSE 1.8% vs 3.4%/2.9%/2.5%) without accompanying confidence intervals, dataset sizes, or ablation references; aligning those numbers to what the text actually supports is load-bearing under a general review.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty framing ('unlike conventional approaches', 'chemistry-agnostic', 'model-free') is core to the paper's positioning; assessing the delta vs the three cited baselines and any omitted contemporaries is a natural read of 'what do you think' applied to a research claim.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you primarily asking whether the technical claims and performance numbers are internally sound and well-supported (soundness / evidence-claim alignment), or whether this work is sufficiently novel and differentiated relative to the cited baselines and the broader literature (novelty / positioning)?
